title: Anacheilium alagoense
uses: Anacheilium alagoense has been reported to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. Its extracts have shown potential in exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, fever, and digestive issues. In traditional Brazilian medicine, the plant is used to treat conditions such as rheumatism and skin problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Anacheilium alagoense for medicinal use.
taxonomy: Anacheilium alagoense belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is classified as a species of orchid. Within this family, it is categorized under the subfamily Epidendroideae. Further classification places it within the tribe Cymbidieae, which includes many species of orchids. Anacheilium alagoense is a genus of orchids, with this specific species being endemic to Brazil. It is a part of the larger group of flowering plants, known as angiosperms.
